description: Per-series dual-source verification log for BDI Raw Data Vault
rule: Every series was confirmed against a minimum of two independent sources before inclusion. Where sources disagreed, the more conservative estimate was used.

series.primary_source: Bureau of Labor Statistics — LNS14000006 (Black) + LNS14000003 (White) via Public Data API v2
series.secondary_source: Economic Policy Institute — State of Working America Data Library (unemployment by race 1972-2024)
series.agreement: Within 0.1pp for all years where both sources provide data
series.conservative_choice: N/A — primary source used; EPI confirms

series.secondary_source: Joint Center for Housing Studies, Harvard — State of the Nation's Housing (homeownership by race tables)
series.agreement: Within 1pp for homeownership rates; income within $500 median

series.primary_source: Federal Reserve Survey of Consumer Finances (SCF) — wealth by race triennial
series.secondary_source: Brookings Institution — Examining the Black-white wealth gap (reproduces SCF with same figures)
series.agreement: Exact match — Brookings draws from same SCF public use data

series.primary_source: NCHS — Health United States Table 4 (life expectancy by race 1900-2019) + NVSR 73(2) 2022
series.secondary_source: CDC WONDER — Underlying Cause of Death (mortality-derived life expectancy by race)
series.agreement: Within 0.1 years for all overlapping periods
series.conservative_choice: NCHS NVSR used as primary — more granular race breakdown

series.primary_source: NCHS — Maternal Mortality Statistics 1915-1960 (historical) + NVSR Vol 73 No 5 (2020-2022)
series.secondary_source: CDC PMSS (Pregnancy Mortality Surveillance System) — Black MMR 2018-2022
series.agreement: 2021 Black MMR: NCHS 69.9 per 100k; CDC PMSS 69.9 — exact match
series.conservative_choice: 2021 figure used for flagship claim (NCHS published)

series.primary_source: Bureau of Justice Statistics — Prisoners Annual (NCJ series)
series.secondary_source: The Sentencing Project — Trends in US Corrections (reproduces BJS with same incarceration figures)
series.agreement: Within 1% for all years where both report
series.conservative_choice: BJS used as primary — direct government source

series.primary_source: Mapping Police Violence (MPV) — comprehensive database of police killings
series.secondary_source: Washington Post Fatal Force database (police shootings by race)
series.agreement: MPV consistently higher than WaPo (WaPo covers shootings only; MPV covers all methods). MPV used as more comprehensive.
series.conservative_choice: MPV used — broader scope; WaPo confirms directional consistency

series.primary_source: NCES — Nation's Report Card (NAEP Long-Term Trend and Main NAEP)
series.secondary_source: Urban Institute — Education Data Portal (reproduces NAEP scale scores by race)
series.agreement: Exact match — Urban Institute draws from NCES data

series.primary_source: Census Bureau — Decennial Census of Housing (1940-2020) + CPS Annual Social and Economic Supplement
series.secondary_source: Harvard JCHS — State of the Nation's Housing (decennial homeownership by race tables)
series.conservative_choice: Census decennial used — most authoritative for homeownership

series.primary_source: CFPB HMDA Data — Home Mortgage Disclosure Act public loan-level data
series.secondary_source: National Community Reinvestment Coalition — HMDA analysis reports
series.agreement: NCRC figures match CFPB raw denial rates within 1pp
series.conservative_choice: CFPB HMDA used as primary — direct regulatory source

series.primary_source: Slave Voyages Database — Emory University (slavevoyages.org, 2023 edition)
series.secondary_source: Eltis & Richardson (2010) — Atlas of the Transatlantic Slave Trade (same underlying database)
series.agreement: Exact match — Atlas uses same database
